events.title: Mexico vs. Ecuador
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Tuesday, June 30, 2026 between Mexico and Ecuador.

markets.question: Will Mexico vs. Ecuador end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 30, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico faces Ecuador in the 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 at Estadio Azteca on June 30, where home advantage, fervent support, and high altitude position El Tri as the narrow favorite in trader pricing. Mexico topped its group with a perfect record, showcasing consistent attacking output and defensive organization, while Ecuador advanced via a resilient comeback victory over Germany that highlighted its counterattacking threat and set-piece efficiency. Head-to-head results heavily favor Mexico across prior encounters, though the South Americans' recent form and physical style create realistic upset potential in a single-elimination setting. The implied probabilities reflect the wisdom of crowds balancing these situational edges against the inherent unpredictability of knockout football.

events.title: What will the announcers say during Mexico vs Ecuador World Cup Match?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by any member of the official FOX broadcasting team during the English broadcast of the Mexico vs Ecuador FIFA World Cup 2026 match on FOX, scheduled for June 30, 2026 at 9 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This market will resolve based on the events of the official match broadcast, beginning from the opening kickoff and ending at the final whistle, including any extra time or penalty shootout, if applicable. Pre-match and post-match commentary will not be considered.

For the purpose of this market, the "FOX broadcasting team" refers to the officially assigned play-by-play announcer, match analyst, sideline reporter, and rules analyst assigned to this match by FOX Sports. Remarks made by other analysts, guest commentators, players, coaches, or any other individuals not part of the officially assigned broadcast team will not count toward resolution.

If the Mexico vs Ecuador match is definitively cancelled, postponed, or otherwise does not air by July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". If this match is interrupted, abbreviated, or only partially aired, this market will resolve according to what has occurred up to that point.

The resolution source for this market is the official English broadcast of the Mexico vs Ecuador FIFA World Cup 2026 match on FOX. Only remarks made live during the official broadcast will count toward this market's resolution. Transcripts will not count.

For full rules, see: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/market_products/Event+Mentions+Contract+DeFi.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico hosts Ecuador in a 2026 FIFA World Cup round of 32 clash at Estadio Azteca on June 30, creating an intense home atmosphere that typically fuels animated announcer calls around Mexican attacking transitions and crowd energy. Mexico enters unbeaten with a clean-sheet run through the group stage, while Ecuador advanced via a late 2-1 upset over Germany that revived their campaign and shifted the bracket. Historical head-to-head results heavily favor El Tri, though both sides feature dynamic forwards capable of producing highlight-reel moments that drive commentary volume. The high-stakes knockout setting, travel for Ecuador, and passionate home support represent the core variables shaping broadcast tone and memorable phrases.

events.title: World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at 18.4% implied probability after opening with a 3-1 victory over Senegal featuring multiple goals from Kylian Mbappé, while Spain sits at 13.8% following a surprising 0-0 draw against Cape Verde that tempered expectations from its Euro 2024-winning squad. England (12.8%) and defending champion Argentina (11.6%) remain tightly clustered behind them after strong early results, including England’s 4-2 win over Croatia and Argentina’s emphatic performances. The bunched pricing among the top five reflects the expanded 48-team format, comparable squad depth and star power across elite European sides, and limited separation in group-stage results so far. Portugal, Brazil, and Germany trail but stay within striking distance as traders weigh recent form, injuries, and knockout-stage matchups that remain weeks away.

events.title: World Cup: Nation To Reach Round of 16
events.description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed team reaches the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 16. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 16 (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after July 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 16 matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Group stage results and remaining fixtures shape advancement odds most directly for any nation eyeing the Round of 16, as the top two teams per group plus select third-place sides advance to the expanded knockout bracket. Recent matches have clarified standings in several groups, with early wins boosting goal difference and points tallies for contenders while leaving others needing favorable results in their final group games. Squad depth, key player fitness from official reports, and historical performance against similar opposition provide additional context traders weigh. The wisdom of crowds reflected in current pricing accounts for these variables alongside schedule difficulty and home-soil advantages for co-hosts, though upsets remain common in tournament play.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup final (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup final matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France sits atop 2026 World Cup winner odds near +400 following convincing group-stage victories anchored by Kylian Mbappé’s multiple-goal contributions, while Spain holds second at roughly +500 despite a surprising draw against Cape Verde. England, Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil round out the leading contenders at +700 to +1000, reflecting trader consensus on squad depth, recent form, and star availability such as Lionel Messi’s hat trick and Jude Bellingham’s influence. Early results have tightened probabilities for European sides with favorable group matchups, while CONMEBOL teams benefit from proven knockout pedigree. Upcoming group fixtures, rest advantages, and potential bracket positioning will shape paths to the final as the expanded 48-team field advances.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Quarterfinals (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after July 21, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup Quarterfinals matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The expanded 48-team format, with 12 groups of four advancing the top two plus eight best third-placed sides to a 32-team knockout stage, has intensified early group-stage competition as of mid-June 2026. Standings after matchday one show tight races in several groups, including draws across Groups G and H that leave qualification wide open, while early winners like Mexico and South Korea in Group A hold narrow leads. Traditional powers such as Brazil, Germany, England, and Argentina benefit from squad depth and recent international form, though host nations gain from home venues and shorter travel. Key upcoming factors include remaining group fixtures through June 27, potential injuries to star players, and how third-place tiebreakers (goal difference, goals scored) resolve the eight additional knockout berths, with round-of-32 and round-of-16 results determining quarterfinalists by July 11.

eventMetadata.context_description: The expanded 48-team 2026 FIFA World Cup format, with 12 groups feeding a 32-team knockout stage and two bracket pathways separating top seeds, shapes trader views on semifinal qualifiers. Spain, France, England, Argentina, Brazil, Portugal, and Germany lead implied probabilities due to deep squads, strong FIFA rankings, and favorable group draws featuring teams like Uruguay, Senegal, and Algeria. Early group-stage results, including Mexico's opening win and South Korea's victory, provide limited signals while highlighting co-host dynamics and rest advantages heading into the Round of 32. Key variables include injury updates to stars like Lamine Yamal or Lionel Messi, historical semifinal experience, and potential upsets from sides advancing as top third-place teams.

events.title: World Cup: Top Scorer (Nation)
events.description:
This market will resolve to the nation that records the most total goals through all rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA World Cup rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the nation who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the nation that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA World Cup rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the nation who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the nation that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany leads the nation-of-top-scorer market at 29.5% implied probability, followed closely by France at 24% and England at 17.5%, reflecting the early World Cup group stage where multiple contenders have already posted multiple goals. Kai Havertz’s brace in Germany’s opening rout, Kylian Mbappé’s continued threat for France, and Harry Kane’s output for England keep those nations bunched, while Spain, Argentina, and Norway sit just behind amid distributed scoring from players like Lionel Messi. The expanded 48-team format, variable group paths, and depth across attacking lines sustain the tight clustering, as no single side has separated decisively in the opening matches.

events.title: World Cup: Nation of Top Goalscorer
events.description:
This market resolves to the nation represented by the player who finishes as the top goalscorer across all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the nation represented by the player who scored fewer goals from penalty kicks. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the nationality of the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no leader declared within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Argentina lead the market at 27.5% each due to star forwards Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi delivering early goals in the 2026 World Cup group stage. Mbappé netted a brace to break France's all-time World Cup scoring record in a win over Senegal, while Messi recorded a hat trick—his first in the tournament—for Argentina against Algeria, placing him atop the Golden Boot standings with three goals. England sits at 19.5% behind Harry Kane's established form and squad depth, and Norway's 9.5% reflects Erling Haaland's two-goal debut performance. These recent results from marquee attackers, combined with deep tournament runs expected for the leading nations, sustain the tight clustering among top contenders.

eventMetadata.context_description: The early stages of the expanded 48-team 2026 FIFA World Cup keep the Fair Play Award race wide open, with dozens of sides still on zero card points after one or two group matches and the award determined by cumulative yellow and red cards through the final. Trader consensus clusters many teams near even implied probability because disciplinary records remain fluid until deeper knockout rounds, when fouls and cautions typically rise. Japan sits atop the field on the strength of its historically low card rates and possession-oriented style that limits defensive infractions, while mid-tier sides such as Ecuador, Uruguay, and Colombia benefit from similarly clean early outings. Established powers like Brazil, Argentina, Spain, and France trade at single-digit levels due to more physical approaches or early cautions, though any team can surge or slip with one contentious match or referee interpretation.

events.title: World Cup: Team with the Most Clean Sheets
events.description:
This market will resolve to the team that records the most clean sheets through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the team that conceded the fewest total goals. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the team that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the team that conceded the fewest total goals. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the team that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 World Cup's most clean sheets market remains tightly contested because multiple teams enter with comparable defensive profiles and favorable early group-stage fixtures against lower-ranked opponents. Spain, France, Germany, England, Argentina, and Ecuador top trader pricing due to strong goalkeepers, organized backlines, and qualifying records of few goals conceded, yet no side has pulled ahead after opening matches. Recent results show several keepers recording shutouts in low-scoring affairs, while the expanded 48-team field and varied group strengths create parity. This setup leaves implied probabilities clustered as the tournament's knockout stages and schedule congestion could shift momentum based on form, injuries, or fixture difficulty.

events.title: World Cup Matches
events.description: This is a market group on individual matches taking place within the 2022 FIFA World Cup. The 2022 FIFA World Cup is the 22nd edition of the World Cup, and is scheduled to be played in Qatar over November 20 - December 18, 2022.

markets.question: World Cup: Tuesday - Will Argentina beat Saudi Arabia?
markets.description:
In the upcoming World Cup soccer game, scheduled for November 22:  

If Argentina wins, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".  

If the game is not completed by December 13, 2022, this market will resolve 50-50.
markets.groupItemTitle: Tues - Will Argentina beat Saudi Arabia?

markets.question: World Cup: Tuesday - Will Denmark beat Tunisia?
markets.description:

events.title: Which country will win the 2022 World Cup?
events.description:
This is a market group on which country's team will win the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

The 2022 FIFA World Cup is the 22nd edition of the World Cup, and is scheduled to be played in Qatar over November 20 - December 18, 2022.

markets.question: Will Brazil win the 2022 World Cup?
markets.description:
In Response to Trader Inquiry: This market may immediately resolve to “No” if the team is eliminated or otherwise has no path to victory based on the rules of the 2022 World Cup.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Brazil is the Champion of FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no Champion of FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 is declared by December 31, 2022, 11:59:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (e.g. https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/qatar2022/scores-fixtures), including official footage from the World Cup, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which countries will qualify from the World Cup Group Stage?
events.description:
The 2022 FIFA World Cup is the 22nd edition of the World Cup, and is scheduled to be played in Qatar over November 20 - December 18, 2022.

In the group stage, teams compete within eight groups composed of four teams each. Eight teams are seeded with the other seeded teams selected using a formula based on the FIFA World Rankings or performances in recent World Cups, and drawn to separate groups. The other teams are assigned to different pots, and teams in each pot are drawn at random to the eight groups.

Each group plays a round-robin tournament, in which each team is scheduled for three matches against other teams in the same group. Thus a total of six matches are played within each group. The top two teams from each group advance to the knockout stage. Points are used to rank the teams within the groups.

markets.question: Will Qatar qualify from Group A?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Qatar qualifies in group A to move on from the group stage to the second round. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If no team from this group has advanced to the second round by December 18, 2022, this market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it can be definitively determined that it will be impossible for this team to move on to the second round, this market will immediately resolve to "No". Likewise, if it can at any point be definitively determined that this team is moving on to the second round, this market will immediately resolve to "Yes".

--

The 2022 FIFA World Cup is the 22nd edition of the World Cup, and is scheduled to be played in Qatar over November 20 - December 18, 2022.

In the group stage, teams compete within eight groups composed of four teams each. Eight teams are seeded with the other seeded teams selected using a formula based on the FIFA World Rankings or performances in recent World Cups, and drawn to separate groups. The other teams are assigned to different pots, and teams in each pot are drawn at random to the eight groups.

Each group plays a round-robin tournament, in which each team is scheduled for three matches against other teams in the same group. Thus a total of six matches are played within each group. The top two teams from each group advance to the knockout stage. Points are used to rank the teams within the groups.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (e.g. https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/qatar2022/scores-fixtures), including official footage from the World Cup, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup: Team to advance to Knockout Stages
events.description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed nation advances to the Knockout Stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the Knockout Stage (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after July 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the Knockout Stage (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after July 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Early group stage results from the expanded 48-team FIFA World Cup 2026 are shaping trader views on advancement probabilities, with 12 groups of four teams each sending the top two plus the eight best third-place finishers to the Round of 32. Recent opening fixtures show strong starts for hosts and favorites, including Mexico's 2-0 win over South Africa and England's 4-2 victory over Croatia featuring goals from Kane and Bellingham, while several groups remain tightly contested after one or two matches per side. Key upcoming factors include remaining group fixtures, goal difference tiebreakers, and squad depth amid a compressed schedule across North American venues. Official FIFA standings and injury reports from national team camps will drive further shifts in implied probabilities as the group phase progresses through late June.

events.title: Winter Games 2026: Countries to win a medal?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country wins one or more medals at the 2026 Milano–Cortina Winter Olympics. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the official medal table as it stands after medals are awarded for the final event of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. Subsequent disqualifications, reallocations, or other changes to medal counts will not be considered.

For the purposes of this market, medals won by athletes competing as Individual Neutral Athletes (INA) will be classified as “INA” and will not count toward any listed nation’s medal total.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are partially completed and then cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve based on medals awarded in completed events.  

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are entirely cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the International Olympic Committee (https://www.olympics.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country wins one or more medals at the 2026 Milano–Cortina Winter Olympics. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the official medal table as it stands after medals are awarded for the final event of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. Subsequent disqualifications, reallocations, or other changes to medal counts will not be considered.

For the purposes of this market, medals won by athletes competing as Individual Neutral Athletes (INA) will be classified as “INA” and will not count toward any listed nation’s medal total.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are partially completed and then cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve based on medals awarded in completed events.  

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are entirely cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the International Olympic Committee (https://www.olympics.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country wins one or more medals at the 2026 Milano–Cortina Winter Olympics. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the official medal table as it stands after medals are awarded for the final event of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. Subsequent disqualifications, reallocations, or other changes to medal counts will not be considered.

For the purposes of this market, medals won by athletes competing as Individual Neutral Athletes (INA) will be classified as “INA” and will not count toward any listed nation’s medal total.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are partially completed and then cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve based on medals awarded in completed events.  

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are entirely cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the International Olympic Committee (https://www.olympics.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed country wins one or more medals at the 2026 Milano–Cortina Winter Olympics. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the official medal table as it stands after medals are awarded for the final event of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. Subsequent disqualifications, reallocations, or other changes to medal counts will not be considered.

For the purposes of this market, medals won by athletes competing as Individual Neutral Athletes (INA) will be classified as “INA” and will not count toward any listed nation’s medal total.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are partially completed and then cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve based on medals awarded in completed events.  

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are entirely cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the International Olympic Committee (https://www.olympics.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve based on the gold medal count from the official medal table as it stands after medals are awarded for the final event of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics. Subsequent disqualifications, reallocations, or other changes to medal counts will not be considered.

For the purposes of this market, medals won by athletes competing as Individual Neutral Athletes (INA) will be classified as “INA” and will not count toward any listed nation’s medal total.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are partially completed and then cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve based on medals awarded in completed events.

If the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics are entirely cancelled or postponed after March 8, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the International Olympic Committee (https://www.olympics.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
